title: Executive cyber security accountability
statement: The board of directors or executive committee is accountable for cyber security.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Executive artificial intelligence accountability
statement: The board of directors or executive committee is accountable for ensuring that artificial intelligence is secure, controllable, human-supervised and used in an ethical and accountable manner.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Cyber security leadership
statement: A chief information security officer provides leadership and oversight of cyber security activities and delivers regular and timely risk-based reporting to the board of directors or executive committee on their organisation’s cyber security posture, the effectiveness of controls, current security risks and emerging cyber threats.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Cyber security resourcing
statement: Suitable and sufficient personnel and resources are identified, acquired and maintained in support of cyber security activities.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Security risk management responsibilities
statement: Security risk management responsibilities for an organisation and their suppliers, partners and customers, including any shared responsibilities, are documented and communicated to all relevant parties with accountability arrangements in place to ensure their effective implementation.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Security risk management assurance
statement: Security risk management activities for an organisation and their systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are embedded into organisational risk management frameworks and subject to ongoing monitoring and assurance activities by the board of directors or executive committee.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Security risk acceptance
statement: Residual security risks for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data), including inherited and shared security risks, are accepted before they are authorised for use and continuously monitored and managed throughout their operational life.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Security risk communication
statement: Residual security risks for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data), including inherited and shared security risks, are transparently and mutually communicated with stakeholders.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: System exposure minimisation
statement: Information about the design, configuration and operation of systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) is not publicly disclosed or shared externally unless necessary for commercial, legal, regulatory or security purposes, with any disclosure minimised, controlled and logged.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Supplier cyber security assurance
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are delivered and supported by trustworthy suppliers whose cyber security practices are independently verified, or otherwise risk-assessed, on a regular basis.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Personnel suitability assurance
statement: Only personnel whose suitability and trustworthiness have been established, and are subject to ongoing assurance, are granted access to systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data).
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Cyber security and safety
statement: Controls for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) do not compromise human, physical or environmental safety.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Legacy system management
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) that are not capable of meeting cyber security requirements are managed using compensating controls, along with enhanced monitoring and assurance activities, to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until they can be decommissioned or replaced.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Continuous cyber security improvement
statement: Security risk management and associated cyber security activities are continually measured and reviewed using cyber threat intelligence and assurance activities, including exercises informed by real-world cyber threats, to identify, prioritise and incorporate improvements in governance arrangements, shared responsibilities and the effectiveness of controls.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Govern cyber security principles

title: Asset identification
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications, identities, credentials and data) are continually and centrally identified and documented.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Asset interdependencies
statement: Interdependencies between systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are continually and centrally identified and documented, including how the compromise of one system could affect the security or business operations of other dependent systems.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Business criticality rating identification
statement: Business criticality ratings for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are identified and documented.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Security requirement identification
statement: Security requirements for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are identified and documented.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Resilience requirement identification
statement: Resilience requirements for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are identified and documented.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Security risk identification
statement: Security risks for an organisation and their systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are identified, including by using current strategic and sector-specific cyber threat intelligence and threat modelling, and are documented along with any associated risk management decisions.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Identify cyber security principles

title: Secure system lifecycle
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems and applications) are planned, designed, developed, tested, deployed, maintained and decommissioned according to their business criticality ratings and security and resilience requirements using Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Cyber supply chain security
statement: Cyber supply chains supporting systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are secure, resilient and support effective and coordinated cyber security incident response.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Identity, credential and access management
statement: Robust and secure identity, credential and access management is used to establish, maintain and control access to systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) and to support effective detection of identity and credential misuse.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Least privilege access
statement: Personnel and services are granted the minimum access to systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) required to undertake their duties.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Secure administration
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are administered in a secure, accountable and auditable manner.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Secure configuration management
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems and applications) are securely configured to approved and maintained baselines, including by reducing attack surfaces and attack paths, with configurations continually monitored and consistently enforced.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Vulnerability management
statement: Vulnerabilities in systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are identified, documented, validated and prioritised for remediation or mitigation in a timely manner, with all remediation and mitigation actions verified for effectiveness.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Trustworthy software
statement: Systems (operating systems and applications) only permit the execution of software that is supported, verified and authorised.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Data protection
statement: Data is encrypted and authenticated at rest and in transit using ASD-approved algorithms and protocols to protect its confidentiality and integrity.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Cryptographic agility
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems and applications) are designed, configured and managed to support timely, prioritised and orderly changes to cryptography, including post-quantum cryptography.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Regular and proven backups
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are backed up in a secure and proven manner on a regular basis, including through the validation of restoration capabilities.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Network segmentation and segregation
statement: Systems (infrastructure, operating systems and applications) are segmented into network zones based on business criticality and trust levels, with only authorised, controlled and monitored inbound and outbound communication paths between network zones permitted.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Operational technology isolation
statement: Operational technology systems (infrastructure) are logically and physically isolated from information technology systems (infrastructure) and all external infrastructure, with only authorised, controlled and monitored communication paths between systems permitted.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Remote access to operational technology
statement: Access to operational technology systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) over untrusted infrastructure is authorised, controlled and monitored.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Content filtering
statement: Data communicated between different security domains for systems (infrastructure and applications) is controlled and subject to inspection and verification.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Cyber security awareness training
statement: Personnel are provided with ongoing cyber security awareness training, including operational security considerations, tailored to their duties, access levels and current cyber threats.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Physical access control
statement: Physical access to facilities and systems (infrastructure and data) is restricted to authorised personnel and monitored for unusual activities.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Protect cyber security principles

title: Centralised event logging
statement: Security-relevant event logs and configuration changes for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are centrally collected, protected against unauthorised modification or deletion, and retained to support effective cyber security event detection and investigation.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Detect cyber security principles

title: Baselined high-risk access activities
statement: Baseline patterns of identity and credential access activities, privileged access activities, and remote access activities are established and maintained for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) to enable the detection of anomalous or unexpected behaviour.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Detect cyber security principles

title: Cyber security event detection
statement: Anomalous or unexpected events and behaviours for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are analysed in a timely manner to detect cyber security events.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Detect cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident identification
statement: Cyber security events are analysed in a timely manner to identify cyber security incidents.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Detect cyber security principles

title: Detection capability efficacy
statement: Cyber security event detection capabilities are regularly evaluated for effectiveness and continuously refined, including by using current strategic and sector-specific cyber threat intelligence, to improve the detection of cyber security events.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Detect cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident planning
statement: Cyber security incident response, business continuity and disaster recovery plans for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) support continued business operations during cyber security incidents, and the resumption of normal business operations following cyber security incidents.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Respond cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident coordination
statement: Cyber security incident roles and responsibilities are defined, documented and exercised to support the internal and external coordination and management of cyber security incidents, including responsibilities for declaring cyber security incidents and undertaking pre-approved response and recovery activities.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Respond cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident response
statement: Cyber security incidents are contained, eradicated and recovered from in a timely manner.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Respond cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident reporting
statement: Cyber security incidents, including associated response and recovery activities, are reported internally and externally to relevant bodies and stakeholders in a timely manner.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Respond cyber security principles

title: Cyber security incident insights
statement: Lessons learnt from cyber security incidents are captured, and areas for improvement are identified, prioritised and actioned in a timely manner.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Respond cyber security principles

title: System recovery assurance
statement: Following cyber security incidents, systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data) are verified through assurance activities to ensure they are secure and capable of supporting the resumption of normal business operations.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Recover cyber security principles

title: Business operations resumption
statement: Residual security risks for systems (infrastructure, operating systems, applications and data), including inherited and shared security risks, are accepted prior to the resumption of normal business operations following cyber security incidents.
group_path: Cyber security principles > The cyber security principles > Recover cyber security principles

title: Control: ism-1997
statement: The board of directors or executive committee defines clear roles and responsibilities for cyber security both within the board of directors or executive committee and broadly within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Embedding cyber security

title: Control: ism-1998
statement: The board of directors or executive committee ensures that cyber security is integrated throughout all business functions within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Embedding cyber security

title: Control: ism-1999
statement: The board of directors or executive committee ensures the cyber security strategy for their organisation is aligned with the overarching strategic direction and business strategy for their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Embedding cyber security

title: Control: ism-2000
statement: The board of directors or executive committee seeks regular briefings or reporting on the cyber security posture of their organisation, as well as the threat environment in which they operate, from internal and external subject matter experts.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Embedding cyber security

title: Control: ism-2001
statement: The board of directors or executive committee champions a positive cyber security culture within their organisation, including through leading by example.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Championing a positive cyber security culture

title: Control: ism-2002
statement: The board of directors or executive committee maintains a sufficient level of cyber security literacy to fulfil both their fiduciary duties and any legislative or regulatory obligations.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Building cyber security expertise

title: Control: ism-2003
statement: The board of directors or executive committee maintains awareness of key cyber security recruitment activities, retention rates for cyber security personnel, and cyber security skills and experience gaps within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Building cyber security expertise

title: Control: ism-2004
statement: The board of directors or executive committee supports the development of cyber security skills and experience for all personnel via internal and external cyber security awareness raising and training opportunities.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Building cyber security expertise

title: Control: ism-2005
statement: The board of directors or executive committee understands the business criticality of their organisation’s systems, including at least a basic understanding of what exists, their value, where they reside, who has access, who might seek access, how they are protected, and how that protection is verified.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Identifying critical business assets

title: Control: ism-2006
statement: The board of directors or executive committee plans for major cyber security incidents, including by participating in exercises, and understand their duties in relation to such cyber security incidents.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Board of directors and executive committee > Planning for major cyber security incidents

title: Control: ism-0714
statement: A CISO is appointed to provide cyber security leadership and guidance for their organisation (covering information technology and operational technology).
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Providing cyber security leadership and guidance

title: Control: ism-1478
statement: The CISO oversees their organisation’s cyber security program and ensures their organisation’s compliance with cyber security policy, standards, regulations and legislation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing the cyber security program

title: Control: ism-1617
statement: The CISO regularly reviews and updates their organisation’s cyber security program to ensure its relevance in addressing cyber threats and harnessing business and cyber security opportunities.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing the cyber security program

title: Control: ism-1966
statement: The CISO develops, implements, maintains and verifies on a regular basis a register of systems used by their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing the cyber security program

title: Control: ism-0724
statement: The CISO implements cyber security measurement metrics and key performance indicators for their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing the cyber security program

title: Control: ism-0725
statement: The CISO coordinates cyber security and business alignment through a cyber security steering committee or advisory board, comprising of key cyber security and business executives, which meets formally and on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Coordinating cyber security

title: Control: ism-0726
statement: The CISO coordinates security risk management activities between cyber security and business teams.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Coordinating cyber security

title: Control: ism-0718
statement: The CISO regularly reports directly to their organisation’s board of directors or executive committee on cyber security matters.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Reporting on cyber security

title: Control: ism-1918
statement: The CISO regularly reports directly to their organisation’s audit, risk and compliance committee (or equivalent) on cyber security matters.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Reporting on cyber security

title: Control: ism-0733
statement: The CISO is fully aware of all cyber security incidents within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing cyber security incident response activities

title: Control: ism-1618
statement: The CISO oversees their organisation’s response to cyber security incidents.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing cyber security incident response activities

title: Control: ism-0734
statement: The CISO contributes to the development, implementation and maintenance of business continuity and disaster recovery plans for their organisation to ensure that business-critical services are supported appropriately in the event of a disaster.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Contributing to business continuity and disaster recovery planning

title: Control: ism-0720
statement: The CISO oversees the development, implementation and maintenance of a cyber security communications strategy to assist in communicating the cyber security vision and strategy for their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Communicating a cyber security vision and strategy

title: Control: ism-0731
statement: The CISO oversees cyber supply chain risk management activities for their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Working with suppliers

title: Control: ism-0732
statement: The CISO receives and manages a dedicated cyber security budget for their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Receiving and managing a dedicated cyber security budget

title: Control: ism-0717
statement: The CISO oversees the management of cyber security personnel within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing cyber security personnel

title: Control: ism-2020
statement: The CISO ensures sufficient cyber security personnel, with the right skills and experience, are acquired to support cyber security activities within their organisation.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing cyber security personnel

title: Control: ism-0735
statement: The CISO oversees the development, implementation and maintenance of their organisation’s cyber security awareness training program.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > Chief information security officer > Overseeing cyber security awareness training

title: Control: ism-1071
statement: Each system has a designated system owner.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > System ownership and oversight

title: Control: ism-1525
statement: System owners register each system with its authorising officer.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > System ownership and oversight

title: Control: ism-1633
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, determine the system boundary, business criticality, and security and resilience objectives for each system based on an assessment of the impact if it were to be compromised or attacked.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1203
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, conduct a threat and risk assessment for each system.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1634
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, select controls for each system and tailor them to achieve desired security and resilience objectives.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-0009
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, identify any supplementary controls required based upon the unique nature of each system, its operating environment and the organisation’s risk tolerances.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1635
statement: System owners implement controls for each system and its operating environment.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1636
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, ensure controls for each non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET system and its operating environment undergo a security assessment by their organisation’s own assessors or Infosec Registered Assessor Program (IRAP) assessors to determine if they have been implemented correctly and are operating as intended.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1967
statement: System owners, in consultation with each system’s authorising officer, ensure controls for each TOP SECRET system and its operating environment, including each sensitive compartmented information system and its operating environment, undergo a security assessment by ASD assessors (or their delegates) to determine if they have been implemented correctly and are operating as intended.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-0027
statement: System owners obtain an authorisation to operate for each non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET system from its authorising officer.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1968
statement: System owners obtain an authorisation to operate for each TOP SECRET system, including for each sensitive compartmented information system, from Director-General ASD (or their delegate).
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1526
statement: System owners continuously monitor the security of each system, and manage associated cyber threats, security risks and controls.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-2021
statement: System owners implement and maintain data minimisation practices for each of their systems.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Protecting systems and their resources

title: Control: ism-1587
statement: System owners report the security status of each system to its authorising officer at least annually.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security roles > System owners > Annual reporting of system security status

title: Control: ism-0576
statement: A cyber security incident management policy, and associated cyber security incident response plan, is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Cyber security incident management policy

title: Control: ism-1784
statement: The cyber security incident management policy, including the associated cyber security incident response plan, is exercised at least annually.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Cyber security incident management policy

title: Control: ism-0125
statement: A cyber security incident register is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Cyber security incident register

title: Control: ism-1803
statement:
A cyber security incident register contains the following for each cyber security incident:

- the date the cyber security incident occurred
- the date the cyber security incident was discovered
- a description of the cyber security incident
- any actions taken in response to the cyber security incident
- to whom the cyber security incident was reported.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Cyber security incident register

title: Control: ism-1625
statement: An insider threat mitigation program is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Insider threat mitigation program

title: Control: ism-1626
statement: Legal advice is sought regarding the development and implementation of an insider threat mitigation program.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Insider threat mitigation program

title: Control: ism-0120
statement: Cyber security personnel have access to sufficient data sources and tools to ensure that systems can be monitored for key indicators of compromise.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Access to sufficient data sources and tools

title: Control: ism-0123
statement: Cyber security incidents are reported to the chief information security officer, or one of their delegates, as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Reporting cyber security incidents

title: Control: ism-0140
statement: Cyber security incidents are reported to ASD as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Reporting cyber security incidents to ASD

title: Control: ism-1880
statement: Cyber security incidents that involve customer data are reported to customers and the public in a timely manner after they occur or are discovered.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Reporting cyber security incidents to customers and the public

title: Control: ism-1881
statement: Cyber security incidents that do not involve customer data are reported to customers and the public in a timely manner after they occur or are discovered.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Managing cyber security incidents > Reporting cyber security incidents to customers and the public

title: Control: ism-1819
statement: Following the identification of a cyber security incident, the cyber security incident response plan is enacted.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Enacting cyber security incident response plans

title: Control: ism-0133
statement: When a data spill occurs, data owners are advised and access to the data is restricted.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing data spills

title: Control: ism-0917
statement:
When malicious code is detected, the following steps are taken to handle the infection:

- the infected systems are isolated
- all previously connected media used in the period leading up to the infection are scanned for signs of infection and isolated if necessary
- antivirus applications are used to remove the infection from infected systems and media
- if the infection cannot be reliably removed, systems are restored from a known good backup or rebuilt.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing malicious code infections

title: Control: ism-1969
statement: Malicious code, when stored or communicated, is treated beforehand to prevent accidental execution.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing malicious code infections

title: Control: ism-1970
statement: Malicious code processed for cyber security incident response or research purposes is done so in a dedicated analysis environment that is segregated from other systems.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing malicious code infections

title: Control: ism-0137
statement: Legal advice is sought before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing intrusions

title: Control: ism-1609
statement: System owners are consulted before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing intrusions

title: Control: ism-1731
statement: Planning and coordination of intrusion remediation activities are conducted on a separate system to that which has been compromised.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing intrusions

title: Control: ism-1732
statement: To the extent possible, all intrusion remediation activities are conducted in a coordinated manner during the same planned outage.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing intrusions

title: Control: ism-1213
statement: Following intrusion remediation activities, full network traffic is captured for at least seven days and analysed to determine whether malicious actors have been successfully removed from the system.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Handling and containing intrusions

title: Control: ism-0138
statement:
The integrity of evidence gathered during an investigation is maintained by investigators:

- recording all of their actions
- maintaining a proper chain of custody
- following all instructions provided by relevant law enforcement agencies.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security incidents > Responding to cyber security incidents > Maintaining the integrity of evidence

title: Control: ism-1631
statement: Suppliers of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services associated with systems are identified.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1452
statement: A supply chain risk assessment is performed for suppliers of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services in order to assess the impact to a system’s security risk profile.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1567
statement: Suppliers identified as high risk by a cyber supply chain risk assessment are not used.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1568
statement: Operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are procured from suppliers that have demonstrated a commitment to the security of their products and services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1882
statement: Operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are procured from suppliers that have demonstrated a commitment to transparency for their products and services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1632
statement: Operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are procured from suppliers that have a strong track record of maintaining the security of their own systems.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1569
statement: A shared responsibility model is created, documented and shared between suppliers and their customers in order to articulate the security responsibilities of each party.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Cyber supply chain risk management activities

title: Control: ism-1785
statement: A supplier relationship management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Supplier relationship management

title: Control: ism-1786
statement: An approved supplier list is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Supplier relationship management

title: Control: ism-1787
statement: Operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are sourced from approved suppliers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Sourcing operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services

title: Control: ism-1788
statement: Multiple potential suppliers are identified for sourcing critical operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Sourcing operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services

title: Control: ism-1789
statement: Sufficient spares of critical IT equipment and OT equipment are sourced and kept in reserve.

title: Control: ism-1791
statement: The integrity of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Delivery of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services

title: Control: ism-1792
statement: The authenticity of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Cyber supply chain risk management > Delivery of operating systems, applications, IT equipment, OT equipment and services

title: Control: ism-1736
statement: A managed service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Managed services

title: Control: ism-1737
statement:
A managed service register contains the following for each managed service:

- managed service provider’s name
- managed service’s name
- purpose for using the managed service
- sensitivity or classification of data involved
- due date for the next security assessment of the managed service
- contractual arrangements for the managed service
- point of contact for users of the managed service
- 24/7 contact details for the managed service provider.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Managed services

title: Control: ism-1793
statement: Managed service providers and their non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET managed services undergo an Infosec Registered Assessor Program (IRAP) assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Assessment of managed service providers

title: Control: ism-1971
statement: Managed service providers and their TOP SECRET managed services, including sensitive compartmented information managed services, undergo a security assessment by ASD assessors (or their delegates), using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the security assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Assessment of managed service providers

title: Control: ism-1637
statement: An outsourced cloud service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Outsourced cloud services

statement:
An outsourced cloud service register contains the following for each outsourced cloud service:

- cloud service provider’s name
- cloud service’s name
- purpose for using the cloud service

- due date for the next security assessment of the cloud service
- contractual arrangements for the cloud service
- point of contact for users of the cloud service
- 24/7 contact details for the cloud service provider.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Outsourced cloud services

title: Control: ism-1529
statement: Only community or private clouds are used for outsourced SECRET and TOP SECRET cloud services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Outsourced cloud services

title: Control: ism-1570
statement: Outsourced cloud service providers and their non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET cloud services undergo an IRAP assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Assessment of outsourced cloud service providers

title: Control: ism-1972
statement: Outsourced cloud service providers and their TOP SECRET cloud services, including sensitive compartmented information cloud services, undergo a security assessment by ASD assessors (or their delegates), using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the security assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Assessment of outsourced cloud service providers

title: Control: ism-1395
statement: Service providers, including any subcontractors, provide an appropriate level of protection for any data entrusted to them or their services.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-0072
statement: Security requirements associated with the confidentiality, integrity and availability of data are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers and reviewed on a regular and ongoing basis to ensure they remain fit for purpose.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1571
statement: The right to verify compliance with security requirements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1738
statement: The right to verify compliance with security requirements documented in contractual arrangements with service providers is exercised on a regular and ongoing basis.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1804
statement: Break clauses associated with failure to meet security requirements are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-0141
statement: The requirement for service providers to report cyber security incidents to a designated point of contact as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1794
statement: A minimum notification period of one month by service providers for significant changes to their own service provider arrangements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1451
statement: Types of data and its ownership is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1572
statement: The regions or availability zones where data will be processed, stored and communicated, as well as a minimum notification period for any configuration changes, is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1573
statement: Access to all logs relating to an organisation’s data and services is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1574
statement: The storage of data in a portable manner that allows for backups, service migration and service decommissioning without any loss of data is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1575
statement: A minimum notification period of one month for the cessation of any services by a service provider is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Contractual security requirements with service providers

title: Control: ism-1073
statement: An organisation’s systems are not accessed or administered by a service provider unless a contractual arrangement exists between the organisation and the service provider to do so.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Access to systems by service providers

title: Control: ism-1576
statement: If an organisation’s systems are accessed or administered by a service provider in an unauthorised manner, the organisation is immediately notified.
group_path: Guidelines for procurement and outsourcing > Managed services and cloud services > Access to systems by service providers

title: Control: ism-0039
statement: A cyber security strategy is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > Development and maintenance of cyber security documentation > Cyber security strategy

title: Control: ism-0047
statement: Organisational-level cyber security documentation is approved by the chief information security officer while system-specific cyber security documentation is approved by the system’s authorising officer.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > Development and maintenance of cyber security documentation > Approval of cyber security documentation

title: Control: ism-1739
statement: A system’s security architecture is approved prior to the development of the system.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > Development and maintenance of cyber security documentation > Approval of cyber security documentation

title: Control: ism-0888
statement: Cyber security documentation is reviewed at least annually and includes a ‘current as at \[date\]’ or equivalent statement.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > Development and maintenance of cyber security documentation > Maintenance of cyber security documentation

title: Control: ism-1602
statement: Cyber security documentation, including notification of subsequent changes, is communicated to all stakeholders.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > Development and maintenance of cyber security documentation > Communication of cyber security documentation

title: Control: ism-0041
statement: Systems have a system security plan that includes an overview of the system (covering the system’s purpose, the system boundary and how the system is managed) as well as an annex that covers applicable controls from this document and any additional controls that have been identified and implemented.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > System security plan

title: Control: ism-0043
statement:
Systems have a cyber security incident response plan that covers the following:

- guidelines on what constitutes a cyber security incident
- the types of cyber security incidents likely to be encountered and the expected response to each type
- how to report cyber security incidents, internally to an organisation and externally to relevant authorities
- other parties which need to be informed in the event of a cyber security incident
- the authority, or authorities, responsible for investigating and responding to cyber security incidents
- the criteria by which an investigation of a cyber security incident would be requested from a law enforcement agency, the Australian Signals Directorate or other relevant authority
- the steps necessary to ensure the integrity of evidence relating to a cyber security incident
- system contingency measures or a reference to such details if they are located in a separate document.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > Cyber security incident response plan

title: Control: ism-0912
statement:
Systems have a change and configuration management plan that includes:

- the establishment and maintenance of authorised baseline configurations for systems
- what constitutes routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- how changes to the configuration of systems will be requested, tracked and documented
- who needs to be consulted prior to routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- who needs to approve routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- who needs to be notified of routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems
- what additional change management and configuration management processes and procedures need to be followed before, during and after routine and urgent changes to the configuration of systems.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > Change and configuration management plan

title: Control: ism-1163
statement:
Systems have a continuous monitoring plan that includes:

- conducting vulnerability scans for systems at least fortnightly
- conducting vulnerability assessments and penetration tests for systems prior to deployment, including prior to deployment of significant changes, and at least annually thereafter
- analysing identified vulnerabilities to determine their potential impact
- implementing mitigations based on risk, effectiveness and cost.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > Continuous monitoring plan

title: Control: ism-1563
statement:
At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a security assessment report is produced by the assessor and covers:

- the scope of the security assessment
- the system’s strengths and weaknesses
- security risks associated with the operation of the system
- the effectiveness of the implementation of controls
- any recommended remediation actions.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > Security assessment report

title: Control: ism-1564
statement: At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a plan of action and milestones is produced by the system owner.
group_path: Guidelines for cyber security documentation > System-specific cyber security documentation > Plan of action and milestones

title: Control: ism-1973
statement: Non-classified systems are secured in suitably secure facilities.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to systems

title: Control: ism-0810
statement: Classified systems are secured in facilities that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their classification.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to systems

title: Control: ism-1974
statement: Non-classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in suitably secure server rooms or communications rooms.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

title: Control: ism-1053
statement: Classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in server rooms or communications rooms that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their classification.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

title: Control: ism-1975
statement: Non-classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in suitably secure security containers.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

title: Control: ism-1530
statement: Classified servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in security containers suitable for their classification taking into account the combination of security zones they reside in.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

title: Control: ism-0813
statement: Server rooms, communications rooms and security containers are not left in unsecured states.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment

group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing radio frequency and infrared devices into facilities

statement: Security measures are used to detect and respond to unauthorised RF devices in SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing radio frequency and infrared devices into facilities

title: Control: ism-2069
statement: An authorised photographic and video recording device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

statement: Unauthorised photographic and video recording devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing photographic and video recording devices into facilities

title: Control: ism-2007
statement: An authorised medical device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing medical devices into facilities

title: Control: ism-2008
statement:
Medical devices that are authorised to be brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas meet, at a minimum, the following criteria:

- are listed on the Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods
- have been prescribed by a legally qualified medical practitioner
- have been commercially purchased within Australia
- do not have inbuilt cellular connectivity
- are capable of operating independently of mobile devices
- where possible, have Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and other forms of wireless connectivity disabled when operating within SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing medical devices into facilities

title: Control: ism-2009
statement: Unauthorised medical devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Bringing medical devices into facilities

title: Control: ism-0164

group_path: Guidelines for physical security > Facilities and systems > Preventing observation by unauthorised people

title: Control: ism-0161
statement: IT equipment and media are secured when not in use.
group_path: Guidelines for physical security > IT equipment and media > Securing IT equipment and media

title: Control: ism-0252
statement:
Cyber security awareness training is undertaken annually by all personnel and covers:

- the purpose of the cyber security awareness training
- security appointments and contacts
- authorised use of systems and their resources
- protection of systems and their resources
- reporting of cyber security incidents and suspected compromises of systems and their resources.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Cyber security awareness training > Providing cyber security awareness training

group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Cyber security awareness training > Providing cyber security awareness training

group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Privileged access to systems

statement: Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Privileged access to systems by foreign nationals

title: Control: ism-0430
statement: Access to systems and their resources are removed or suspended the same day personnel no longer have a legitimate requirement for access.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Suspension of access to systems

title: Control: ism-1591
statement: Access to systems and their resources are removed or suspended as soon as practicable when personnel are detected undertaking malicious activities.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Suspension of access to systems

title: Control: ism-1404

group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Suspension of access to systems

title: Control: ism-1648
statement: Privileged access to systems and their resources are disabled after 45 days of inactivity.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Suspension of access to systems

title: Control: ism-1647
statement: Privileged access to systems and their resources are disabled after 12 months unless revalidated.
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Suspension of access to systems

title: Control: ism-0407
statement:
A secure record is maintained for the life of systems and their resources that covers the following for each user:

- their user identification
- their signed agreement to abide by system usage policies
- who authorised their access
- when their access was granted
- the level of access they were granted
- when their access, and their level of access, was last reviewed
- when their level of access was changed, and to what extent (if applicable)
- when their access was withdrawn (if applicable).
group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Recording authorisation for personnel to access systems

title: Control: ism-0441

group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Temporary access to systems

statement: Break glass accounts are only used when normal authentication processes cannot be used.

group_path: Guidelines for personnel security > Access to systems and their resources > Emergency access to systems

statement: A mobile device usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

title: Control: ism-1083
statement: Personnel are advised of the sensitivity or classification permitted for voice and data communications when using mobile devices.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > Personnel awareness

title: Control: ism-1299
statement:
Personnel are advised to take the following precautions when using mobile devices:

- never leave mobile devices or removable media unattended, including by placing them in checked-in luggage or leaving them in hotel safes
- never store credentials with mobile devices that they grant access to, such as in laptop computer bags
- never lend mobile devices or removable media to untrusted people, even if briefly
- never allow untrusted people to connect their mobile devices or removable media to your mobile devices, including for charging
- never connect mobile devices to designated charging stations or wall outlet charging ports
- never use gifted or unauthorised peripherals, chargers or removable media with mobile devices
- never use removable media for data transfers or backups that have not been checked for malicious code beforehand
- avoid reuse of removable media once used with other parties’ systems or mobile devices
- avoid connecting mobile devices to open or untrusted Wi-Fi networks
- consider disabling any communications capabilities of mobile devices when not in use, such as Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Near Field Communication and ultra-wideband
- consider periodically rebooting mobile devices
- consider using a VPN connection to encrypt all cellular and wireless communications
- consider using encrypted email or messaging apps for all communications.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > Personnel awareness

title: Control: ism-0240

group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > Using paging, message services and messaging apps

title: Control: ism-1298
