title: Control: ism-1554
statement:
If travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel are:

- issued with newly provisioned user accounts, mobile devices and removable media from a pool of dedicated travel devices which are used solely for work-related activities
- advised on how to apply and inspect tamper seals to key areas of mobile devices
- advised to avoid taking any personal mobile devices, especially if rooted or jailbroken.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > Before travelling overseas with mobile devices

title: Control: ism-1555
statement:
Before travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions:

- record all details of the mobile devices being taken, such as product types, serial numbers and International Mobile Equipment Identity numbers
- update all operating systems and applications
- remove all non-essential data, applications and user accounts
- backup all remaining data, applications and settings.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > Before travelling overseas with mobile devices

title: Control: ism-1088
statement:
Personnel report the potential compromise of mobile devices, removable media or credentials to their organisation as soon as possible, especially if they:

- provide credentials to foreign government officials
- decrypt mobile devices for foreign government officials
- have mobile devices taken out of sight by foreign government officials
- have mobile devices or removable media stolen, including if later returned
- lose mobile devices or removable media, including if later found
- observe unusual behaviour of mobile devices.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > While travelling overseas with mobile devices

title: Control: ism-1300
statement:
Upon returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions:

- sanitise and reset mobile devices, including all removable media
- decommission any credentials that left their possession during their travel
- report if significant doubt exists as to the integrity of any mobile devices or removable media.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > After travelling overseas with mobile devices

title: Control: ism-1556
statement:
If returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel take the following additional actions:

- reset credentials used with mobile devices, including those used for remote access to their organisation’s systems
- monitor user accounts for any indicators of compromise, such as failed logon attempts.
group_path: Guidelines for enterprise mobility > Mobile device usage > After travelling overseas with mobile devices

title: Control: ism-0280
statement: If procuring an evaluated product, a product that has completed a PP-based evaluation, including against all applicable PP modules (as well as a software bill of materials assessment if applicable), is selected in preference to one that has completed an EAL-based evaluation.
group_path: Guidelines for evaluated products > Evaluated product procurement > Evaluated product selection

title: Control: ism-0285
statement: Evaluated products are delivered in a manner consistent with any delivery procedures defined in associated evaluation documentation.
group_path: Guidelines for evaluated products > Evaluated product procurement > Delivery of evaluated products

title: Control: ism-0286
statement: When procuring high assurance information technology (IT) equipment, ASD is contacted for any equipment-specific delivery procedures.
group_path: Guidelines for evaluated products > Evaluated product procurement > Delivery of evaluated products

title: Control: ism-0289
statement: Evaluated products are installed, configured, administered and operated in an evaluated configuration and in accordance with vendor guidance.
group_path: Guidelines for evaluated products > Evaluated product usage > Using evaluated products

title: Control: ism-0290
statement: High assurance IT equipment is installed, configured, administered and operated in an evaluated configuration and in accordance with ASD guidance.
group_path: Guidelines for evaluated products > Evaluated product usage > Using evaluated products

title: Control: ism-1551
statement: An IT equipment management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > IT equipment management policy

title: Control: ism-1913
statement: Approved configurations for IT equipment are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Hardening IT equipment configurations

title: Control: ism-1858
statement: IT equipment is hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Hardening IT equipment configurations

title: Control: ism-0336
statement: A networked IT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > IT equipment registers

title: Control: ism-1869
statement: A non-networked IT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > IT equipment registers

title: Control: ism-0294
statement: IT equipment, with the exception of high assurance IT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Labelling IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0296
statement: ASD’s approval is sought before applying labels to external surfaces of high assurance IT equipment.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Labelling high assurance IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0293
statement: IT equipment is classified based on the highest sensitivity or classification of data that it is approved for processing, storing or communicating.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Classifying IT equipment

title: Control: ism-1599
statement: IT equipment is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment usage > Handling IT equipment

title: Control: ism-1079
statement: ASD’s approval is sought before undertaking any maintenance or repairs to high assurance IT equipment.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > Maintenance and repairs of high assurance IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0305
statement: Maintenance and repairs of IT equipment is carried out on site by an appropriately cleared technician.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > On-site maintenance and repairs

title: Control: ism-0307
statement: If an appropriately cleared technician is not used to undertake maintenance or repairs of IT equipment, the IT equipment and associated media is sanitised before maintenance or repair work is undertaken.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > On-site maintenance and repairs

title: Control: ism-0306
statement:
If an appropriately cleared technician is not used to undertake maintenance or repairs of IT equipment, the technician is escorted by someone who:

- is appropriately cleared and briefed
- takes due care to ensure that data is not disclosed
- takes all responsible measures to ensure the integrity of the IT equipment
- has the authority to direct the technician
- is sufficiently familiar with the IT equipment to understand the work being performed.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > On-site maintenance and repairs

title: Control: ism-0310
statement: IT equipment maintained or repaired off site is done so at facilities approved for handling the sensitivity or classification of the IT equipment.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > Off-site maintenance and repairs

title: Control: ism-1598
statement: Following maintenance or repair activities for IT equipment, the IT equipment is inspected to confirm it retains its approved configuration and that no unauthorised modifications have taken place.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment maintenance and repairs > Inspection of IT equipment following maintenance and repairs

title: Control: ism-0313
statement: IT equipment sanitisation processes, and supporting IT equipment sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > IT equipment sanitisation processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-1741
statement: IT equipment destruction processes, and supporting IT equipment destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > IT equipment destruction processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-0311
statement: IT equipment containing media is sanitised by removing the media from the IT equipment or by sanitising the media in situ.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising IT equipment

title: Control: ism-1742
statement: IT equipment that cannot be sanitised is destroyed.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising IT equipment

title: Control: ism-1218
statement: IT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data, is sanitised in situ.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising highly sensitive IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0312
statement: IT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data that cannot be sanitised in situ, is returned to Australia for destruction.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising highly sensitive IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0315
statement: High assurance IT equipment is destroyed prior to its disposal.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Destroying high assurance IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0317
statement: At least three pages of random text with no blank areas are printed on each colour printer cartridge or MFD print drum.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-1219
statement: MFD print drums and image transfer rollers are inspected and destroyed if there is remnant toner which cannot be removed or a print is visible on the image transfer roller.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-1220
statement: Printer and MFD platens are inspected and destroyed if any text or images are retained on the platen.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-1221
statement: Printers and MFDs are checked to ensure no pages are trapped in the paper path due to a paper jam.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-0318
statement: When unable to sanitise printer cartridges or MFD print drums, they are destroyed as per electrostatic memory devices.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-1534
statement: Printer ribbons in printers and MFDs are removed and destroyed.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising printers and multifunction devices

title: Control: ism-1076
statement: Televisions and computer monitors with minor burn-in or image persistence are sanitised by displaying a solid white image on the screen for an extended period of time.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising televisions and computer monitors

title: Control: ism-1222
statement: Televisions and computer monitors that cannot be sanitised are destroyed.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising televisions and computer monitors

title: Control: ism-1223
statement:
Memory in network devices is sanitised using the following processes, in order of preference:

- following device-specific guidance provided in evaluation documentation
- following vendor sanitisation guidance
- loading a dummy configuration file, performing a factory reset and then reinstalling firmware.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment sanitisation and destruction > Sanitising network devices

title: Control: ism-1550
statement: IT equipment disposal processes, and supporting IT equipment disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment disposal > IT equipment disposal processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-1217
statement: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate IT equipment with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment disposal > Disposal of IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0321
statement: When disposing of IT equipment that has been designed or modified to meet emanation security standards, ASD is contacted for requirements relating to its disposal.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment disposal > Disposal of IT equipment

title: Control: ism-0316
statement: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release IT equipment, or its waste, into the public domain.
group_path: Guidelines for information technology equipment > IT equipment disposal > Disposal of IT equipment

title: Control: ism-1549
statement: A media management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Media management policy

title: Control: ism-1359
statement: A removable media usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Removable media usage policy

title: Control: ism-1713
statement: A removable media register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Removable media register

title: Control: ism-0332
statement: Media, with the exception of internally mounted fixed media within information technology equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Labelling media

title: Control: ism-0323
statement: Media is classified to the highest sensitivity or classification of data it stores, unless the media has been classified to a higher sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Classifying media

title: Control: ism-0337
statement: Media is only used with systems that are authorised to process, store or communicate its sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Classifying media

title: Control: ism-0325
statement: Any media connected to a system with a higher sensitivity or classification than the media is reclassified to the higher sensitivity or classification, unless the media is read-only or the system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Reclassifying media

title: Control: ism-0330
statement: Before reclassifying media to a lower sensitivity or classification, the media is sanitised or destroyed, and a formal administrative decision is made to reclassify it.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Reclassifying media

title: Control: ism-0831
statement: Media is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Handling media

title: Control: ism-1059
statement: All data stored on media is encrypted.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Handling media

title: Control: ism-1600
statement: Media is sanitised before it is used for the first time.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Sanitising media before first use

title: Control: ism-1642
statement: Media is sanitised before it is reused in a different security domain.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Sanitising media before first use

title: Control: ism-0347
statement: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, write-once media is used unless the destination system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Using media for data transfers

title: Control: ism-0947
statement: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, rewritable media is sanitised after each data transfer.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media usage > Using media for data transfers

title: Control: ism-0348
statement: Media sanitisation processes, and supporting media sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Media sanitisation processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-0351
statement: Volatile media is sanitised by removing its power for at least 10 minutes.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Volatile media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0352
statement: SECRET and TOP SECRET volatile media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Volatile media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0835
statement: Following sanitisation, TOP SECRET volatile media retains its classification if it stored static data for an extended period of time, or had data repeatedly stored on or written to the same memory location for an extended period of time.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Treatment of volatile media following sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0354
statement: Non-volatile magnetic media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once (or three times if pre-2001 or under 15 GB) in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile magnetic media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-1065
statement: The host-protected area and device configuration overlay table are reset prior to the sanitisation of non-volatile magnetic hard drives.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile magnetic media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-1067
statement: The ATA secure erase command is used, in addition to block overwriting software, to ensure the growth defects table of non-volatile magnetic hard drives is overwritten.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile magnetic media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0356
statement: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile magnetic media retains its classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Treatment of non-volatile magnetic media following sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0357
statement: Non-volatile EPROM media is sanitised by applying three times the manufacturer’s specified ultraviolet erasure time and then overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile erasable programmable read-only memory media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0836
statement: Non-volatile EEPROM media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile electrically erasable programmable read-only memory media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0358
statement: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile EPROM and EEPROM media retains its classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Treatment of non-volatile erasable and electrically erasable programmable read-only memory media following sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0359
statement: Non-volatile flash memory media is sanitised by overwriting it at least twice in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Non-volatile flash memory media sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0360
statement: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile flash memory media retains its classification.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Treatment of non-volatile flash memory media following sanitisation

title: Control: ism-1735
statement: Media that cannot be successfully sanitised is destroyed prior to its disposal.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media sanitisation > Media that cannot be successfully sanitised

title: Control: ism-0363
statement: Media destruction processes, and supporting media destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-0350
statement:
The following media types are destroyed prior to their disposal:

- microfiche and microfilm
- optical discs
- programmable read-only memory
- read-only memory
- other types of media that cannot be sanitised.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media that cannot be sanitised

title: Control: ism-1361
statement: Security Construction and Equipment Committee-approved equipment or ASIO-approved equipment is used when destroying media.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction equipment

title: Control: ism-1160
statement: If using degaussers to destroy media, degaussers evaluated by the United States’ National Security Agency are used.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction equipment

title: Control: ism-1517
statement: Equipment that is capable of reducing microform to a fine powder, with resultant particles not showing more than five consecutive characters per particle upon microscopic inspection, is used to destroy microfiche and microfilm.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1722
statement: Electrostatic memory devices are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator or grinder/sander.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1723
statement: Magnetic floppy disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1724
statement: Magnetic hard disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or degausser.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1725
statement: Magnetic tapes are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1726
statement: Optical disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1727
statement: Semiconductor memory is destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill or disintegrator.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-0368
statement: Media destroyed using a hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting results in media waste particles no larger than 9 mm.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Media destruction methods

title: Control: ism-1728
statement: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, PROTECTED if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 6 mm, or SECRET if greater than 6 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Treatment of media waste particles

title: Control: ism-1729
statement: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of TOP SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, or SECRET if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Treatment of media waste particles

title: Control: ism-0361
statement: Magnetic media is destroyed using a degausser with a suitable magnetic field strength and magnetic orientation.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Degaussing magnetic media

title: Control: ism-0362
statement: Product-specific directions provided by degausser manufacturers are followed.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Degaussing magnetic media

title: Control: ism-1641
statement: Following the use of a degausser, magnetic media is physically damaged by deforming any internal platters.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Degaussing magnetic media

title: Control: ism-0370
statement: The destruction of media is performed under the supervision of at least one cleared person.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Supervision of destruction

title: Control: ism-0371
statement: Personnel supervising the destruction of media supervise its handling to the point of destruction and ensure that the destruction is completed successfully.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Supervision of destruction

title: Control: ism-0372
statement: The destruction of media storing accountable material is performed under the supervision of at least two cleared personnel.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Supervision of accountable material destruction

title: Control: ism-0373
statement: Personnel supervising the destruction of media storing accountable material supervise its handling to the point of destruction, ensure that the destruction is completed successfully and sign a destruction certificate afterwards.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Supervision of accountable material destruction

title: Control: ism-0839
statement: The destruction of media storing accountable material is not outsourced.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Outsourcing media destruction

title: Control: ism-0840
statement: When outsourcing the destruction of media storing non-accountable material, a National Association for Information Destruction AAA certified destruction service with endorsements, as specified in ASIO’s Protective Security Circular-167, is used.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media destruction > Outsourcing media destruction

title: Control: ism-0374
statement: Media disposal processes, and supporting media disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media disposal > Media disposal processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-0378
statement: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate media with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media disposal > Disposal of media

title: Control: ism-0375
statement: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release media, or its waste, into the public domain.
group_path: Guidelines for media > Media disposal > Disposal of media

title: Control: ism-1743
statement: Vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to Secure by Design and Secure by Default principles and practices, including secure programming practices and either memory-safe programming languages or less preferably memory-safe programming practices, are used for operating systems.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Operating system selection

title: Control: ism-1407
statement: The latest release, or the previous release, of operating systems are used.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Operating system releases and versions

title: Control: ism-1408
statement: Where supported, 64-bit versions of operating systems are used.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Operating system releases and versions

title: Control: ism-1406
statement: SOEs are used for workstations and servers.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Standard Operating Environments

title: Control: ism-1608
statement: SOEs provided by third parties are scanned for malicious code and configurations.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Standard Operating Environments

title: Control: ism-1588
statement: SOEs are reviewed and updated at least annually.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Standard Operating Environments

title: Control: ism-1914
statement: Approved configurations for operating systems are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1409
statement: Operating systems are hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance, with the most restrictive guidance taking precedence when conflicts occur.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-0383
statement: Default user accounts or credentials for operating systems, including for any pre-configured user accounts, are changed, disabled or removed during initial setup.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-0380
statement: Unneeded user accounts, components, services and functionality of operating systems are disabled or removed.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-0341
statement: Automatic execution features for removable media are disabled.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1654
statement: Internet Explorer 11 is disabled or removed.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1655
statement: .NET Framework 3.5 (includes .NET 2.0 and 3.0) is disabled or removed.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1492
statement: Operating system exploit protection functionality is enabled.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1745
statement: Early Launch Antimalware, Secure Boot, Trusted Boot and Measured Boot functionality is enabled.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1584
statement: Unprivileged users are prevented from bypassing, disabling or modifying security functionality of operating systems.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1491
statement:
Unprivileged users are prevented from running script execution engines, including:

- Windows Script Host (cscript.exe and wscript.exe)
- PowerShell (powershell.exe, powershell_ise.exe and pwsh.exe)
- Command Prompt (cmd.exe)
- Windows Management Instrumentation (wmic.exe)
- Microsoft Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) Application Host (mshta.exe).
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Hardening operating system configurations

title: Control: ism-1592
statement: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to install unapproved applications.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application management

title: Control: ism-0382
statement: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to uninstall or disable approved applications.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application management

title: Control: ism-0843
statement: Application control is implemented on workstations.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1490
statement: Application control is implemented on internet-facing servers.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1656
statement: Application control is implemented on non-internet-facing servers.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1870
statement: Application control is applied to user profiles and temporary folders used by operating systems, web browsers and email clients.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1871
statement: Application control is applied to all locations other than user profiles and temporary folders used by operating systems, web browsers and email clients.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1657
statement: Application control restricts the execution of executables, libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications and control panel applets to an organisation-approved set.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1658
statement: Application control restricts the execution of drivers to an organisation-approved set.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-0955
statement: Application control is implemented using cryptographic hash rules, publisher certificate rules or path rules.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1471
statement: When implementing application control using publisher certificate rules, publisher names and product names are used.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1392
statement: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can modify approved files and write to approved folders.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1746
statement: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can change file system permissions for approved files and folders.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1544
statement: Microsoft’s recommended application blocklist is implemented.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1659
statement: Microsoft’s vulnerable driver blocklist is implemented.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1582
statement: Application control rulesets are validated on an annual or more frequent basis.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-0846
statement: All users (with the exception of local administrator accounts and break glass accounts) cannot disable, bypass or be exempted from application control.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1660
statement: Allowed and blocked application control events are centrally logged.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Application control

title: Control: ism-1889
statement: Command line process creation events are centrally logged.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Command Shell

title: Control: ism-1621
statement: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled or removed.

title: Control: ism-1417
statement:
An antivirus application is implemented on workstations and servers with:

- signature-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level
- heuristic-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level
- reputation rating functionality enabled
- ransomware protection functionality enabled
- detection signatures configured to update on at least a daily basis
- regular scanning configured for all fixed disks and removable media.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Operating system hardening > Antivirus application

statement: Multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate users to third-party online customer services that process, store or communicate their organisation’s sensitive customer data.

statement: Multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate customers to online customer services that process, store or communicate sensitive customer data.

statement: When multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate users or customers to online services or online customer services, all other authentication protocols that do not support multi-factor authentication are disabled.

title: Control: ism-1873
statement: Multi-factor authentication used for authenticating customers of online customer services provides a phishing-resistant option.

statement: Multi-factor authentication used for authenticating customers of online customer services is phishing-resistant.

statement: Passwords used for multi-factor authentication on SECRET systems are a minimum of 8 characters.

group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

title: Control: ism-0421
statement: Passwords used for single-factor authentication on non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED systems are a minimum of 15 characters.

title: Control: ism-1557
statement: Passwords used for single-factor authentication on SECRET systems are a minimum of 17 characters.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

statement: Passwords used for single-factor authentication on TOP SECRET systems are a minimum of 20 characters.

title: Control: ism-1558
statement:
Passwords using a sequence of words for single-factor authentication are not constructed using:

- a list of categorised words
- a real sentence in a natural language
- song lyrics, movie or television show quotes, literature, or any other publicly available material
- less than 4 random words for non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive and PROTECTED systems; 5 random words for SECRET systems; or 6 random words for TOP SECRET systems.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

statement: Maximum length limits for passwords are not less than 64 characters.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

title: Control: ism-2080
statement: Password complexity requirements are not imposed for passwords.

title: Control: ism-2081

group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Password strength

title: Control: ism-1590
statement:
Credentials for user accounts are changed if:

- they are compromised
- they are suspected of being compromised
- they are discovered stored on networks in the clear
- they are discovered being transferred across networks in the clear
- membership of a shared user account changes.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Changing credentials

title: Control: ism-1955
statement: Credentials for computer accounts are changed if they are compromised, they are suspected of being compromised or they have not been changed in the past 30 days.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Changing credentials

title: Control: ism-1847
statement: Credentials for the Kerberos Key Distribution Center’s service account (KRBTGT) are changed twice, allowing for replication to all Microsoft AD DS domain controllers in-between each change, if the domain has been directly compromised, the domain is suspected of being compromised or they have not been changed in the past 12 months.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Changing credentials

title: Control: ism-1956
statement: Microsoft AD FS token-signing and encryption certificates are changed twice in quick succession if they are compromised, they are suspected of being compromised or they have not been changed in the past 12 months.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Changing credentials

title: Control: ism-1403
statement: User accounts, except for break glass accounts, are locked out after a maximum of five failed logon attempts.

title: Control: ism-0853
statement: On a daily basis, outside of business hours and after an appropriate period of inactivity, user sessions are terminated and workstations are restarted.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Session termination

title: Control: ism-0428
statement:
Services are configured with a session lock that:

- activates after a maximum of 15 minutes of user inactivity, a maximum of 12 hours of overall session time or when manually activated by users
- blocks access to all session content
- requires users to re-authenticate using all authentication factors to unlock the session
- denies users the ability to disable the session locking mechanism.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Session locking

title: Control: ism-2012
statement:
Systems are configured with a screen lock that:

- activates after a maximum of 15 minutes of user inactivity, or when manually activated by users
- conceals all content on the screen
- ensures that the screen does not enter a power saving state before the screen lock is activated
- requires users to re-authenticate using all authentication factors to unlock the system
- denies users the ability to disable the screen locking mechanism.
group_path: Guidelines for system hardening > Authentication hardening > Screen locking

title: Control: ism-0408

title: Control: ism-1460

title: Control: ism-1604

title: Control: ism-1537
statement:
Security-relevant events for databases are centrally logged, including:

- access or modification of particularly important content
- addition of new users, especially privileged users
- changes to user roles or privileges
- attempts to elevate user privileges
- queries containing comments
- queries containing multiple embedded queries
- database and query alerts or failures
- database structure changes
- database administrator actions
- use of executable commands
- database logons and logoffs.
group_path: Guidelines for database systems > Databases > Database event logging

title: Control: ism-1431
statement:
Denial-of-service attack mitigation strategies are discussed with cloud service providers, specifically:

- their capacity to withstand denial-of-service attacks
- costs likely to be incurred as a result of denial-of-service attacks
- availability monitoring and thresholds for notification of denial-of-service attacks
- thresholds for turning off any online services or functionality during denial-of-service attacks
- pre-approved actions that can be undertaken during denial-of-service attacks
- any arrangements with upstream service providers to block malicious network traffic as far upstream as possible.
group_path: Guidelines for networking > Service continuity for online services > Denial-of-service attack mitigation strategies

title: Control: ism-0484
statement:
The SSH daemon is configured to:

- only listen on the required interfaces (ListenAddress xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx)
- have a suitable login banner (Banner x)
- have a login authentication timeout of no more than 60 seconds (LoginGraceTime 60)
- disable host-based authentication (HostbasedAuthentication no)
- disable rhosts-based authentication (IgnoreRhosts yes)
- disable the ability to login directly as root (PermitRootLogin no)
- disable empty passwords (PermitEmptyPasswords no)
- disable connection forwarding (AllowTCPForwarding no)
- disable gateway ports (GatewayPorts no)
- disable X11 forwarding (X11Forwarding no).
group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Secure Shell > Configuring Secure Shell

title: Control: ism-0485
statement: Public key-based authentication is used for SSH connections.

- access from IP addresses that do not require access
- port forwarding
- agent credential forwarding
- X11 forwarding
- console access.
group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Secure Shell > Automated remote access

title: Control: ism-0488

group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Internet Protocol Security > Integrity algorithms

title: Control: ism-0999
statement: DH or ECDH is used for key establishment of IPsec connections, preferably 384-bit random ECP group, 3072-bit MODP Group or 4096-bit MODP Group.
group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Internet Protocol Security > Diffie-Hellman groups

title: Control: ism-0498
statement: A security association lifetime of less than four hours (14400 seconds) is used for IPsec connections.
group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Internet Protocol Security > Security association lifetimes

title: Control: ism-1000
statement: PFS is used for IPsec connections.
group_path: Guidelines for cryptography > Internet Protocol Security > Perfect Forward Secrecy

title: Control: ism-0628
statement: Gateways are implemented between networks belonging to different security domains.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Implementing gateways

title: Control: ism-0637
statement: Gateways implement a demilitarised zone if external parties require access to an organisation’s services.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Implementing gateways

title: Control: ism-0631
statement: Gateways only allow explicitly authorised data flows.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Implementing gateways

title: Control: ism-1192
statement: Gateways inspect and filter data flows at the transport and above network layers.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Implementing gateways

title: Control: ism-1427
statement: Gateways perform ingress traffic filtering to detect and prevent IP source address spoofing.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Implementing gateways

title: Control: ism-1520
statement: System administrators for gateways undergo appropriate employment screening, and where necessary hold an appropriate security clearance, based on the sensitivity or classification of gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-0613
statement: System administrators for gateways that connect to Australian Eyes Only or Releasable To networks are Australian nationals.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-1773
statement: System administrators for gateways that connect to Australian Government Access Only networks are Australian nationals or seconded foreign nationals.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-0611
statement: System administrators for gateways are assigned the minimum privileges required to perform their duties.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-0616
statement: Separation of duties is implemented in performing administrative activities for gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-0612
statement: System administrators for gateways are formally trained on the operation and management of gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administrators for gateways

title: Control: ism-1774
statement: Gateways are managed via a secure path isolated from all connected networks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administration of gateways

title: Control: ism-0629
statement: For gateways between networks belonging to different security domains, any shared components are managed by system administrators for the higher security domain or by system administrators from a mutually agreed upon third party.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > System administration of gateways

title: Control: ism-0619
statement: Users authenticate to other networks accessed via gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Authenticating to networks accessed via gateways

title: Control: ism-0622
statement: IT equipment authenticates to other networks accessed via gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Authenticating to networks accessed via gateways

title: Control: ism-1783
statement: Public IP addresses controlled by, or used by, an organisation are signed by valid ROA records.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Border Gateway Protocol routing security

title: Control: ism-2018
statement: Routes for RPKI-registered IP addresses that are advertised from invalid Autonomous Systems, or that are longer than allowed, are rejected or deprioritised by routers that exchange routes via BGP.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Border Gateway Protocol routing security

title: Control: ism-0634
statement:
Security-relevant events for gateways are centrally logged, including:

- data packets and data flows permitted through gateways
- data packets and data flows attempting to leave gateways
- real-time alerts for attempted intrusions.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Gateway event logging

title: Control: ism-1037
statement: Gateways undergo testing following configuration changes, and at regular intervals no more than six months apart, to validate they conform to expected security configurations.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Assessment of gateways

title: Control: ism-0100
statement: Non-classified, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, PROTECTED and SECRET gateways undergo an IRAP assessment, using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the IRAP assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Assessment of gateways

title: Control: ism-2019
statement: TOP SECRET gateways undergo a security assessment by ASD assessors (or their delegates), using the latest release of the ISM available prior to the beginning of the assessment (or a subsequent release), at least every 24 months.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Gateways > Assessment of gateways

title: Control: ism-0626
statement: CDSs are implemented between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and any other networks belonging to different security domains.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Implementing Cross Domain Solutions

title: Control: ism-0597
statement: When planning, designing, implementing or introducing additional connectivity to CDSs, ASD is consulted and any directions provided by ASD are complied with.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Consultation on Cross Domain Solutions

title: Control: ism-0635
statement: CDSs implement isolated upward and downward network paths.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Separation of data flows

title: Control: ism-1522
statement: CDSs implement independent security-enforcing functions for upward and downward network paths.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Separation of data flows

title: Control: ism-1521
statement: CDSs implement protocol breaks at each network layer.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Separation of data flows

title: Control: ism-0670
statement: Security-relevant events for CDSs are centrally logged.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Cross Domain Solution event logging

title: Control: ism-1523
statement: A sample of security-relevant events relating to data transfer policies are taken at least every three months and assessed against security policies for CDSs to identify any operational failures.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > Cross Domain Solution event logging

title: Control: ism-0610
statement: Users are trained on the secure use of CDSs before access is granted.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Cross Domain Solutions > User training

title: Control: ism-1528
statement: Evaluated firewalls are used between an organisation’s networks and public network infrastructure.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Firewalls > Using firewalls

title: Control: ism-0639
statement: Evaluated firewalls are used between networks belonging to different security domains.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Firewalls > Using firewalls

title: Control: ism-1862
statement: If using a WAF, disclosing the IP addresses of web servers under an organisation’s control (referred to as origin servers) is avoided and access to the origin servers is restricted to the WAF and authorised management networks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web application firewalls > Using web application firewalls

title: Control: ism-0643
statement: Evaluated diodes are used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between an organisation’s networks and public network infrastructure.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Diodes > Using diodes

title: Control: ism-0645
statement: Evaluated diodes used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and public network infrastructure complete a high assurance evaluation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Diodes > Using diodes

title: Control: ism-1157
statement: Evaluated diodes are used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between networks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Diodes > Using diodes

title: Control: ism-1158
statement: Evaluated diodes used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and any other networks complete a high assurance evaluation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Diodes > Using diodes

title: Control: ism-0260
statement: All web access, including that by internal servers, is conducted through web proxies.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web proxies > Using web proxies

title: Control: ism-0261
statement:
The following details are centrally logged for websites accessed via web proxies:

- web address
- date and time
- user
- amount of data uploaded and downloaded
- internal and external IP addresses.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web proxies > Web proxy event logging

title: Control: ism-0963
statement: Web content filtering is implemented to filter potentially harmful web-based content.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Using web content filters

title: Control: ism-0961
statement: Client-side active content is restricted by web content filters to an organisation-approved list of domain names.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Using web content filters

title: Control: ism-1237
statement: Web content filtering is applied to outbound web traffic where appropriate.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Using web content filters

title: Control: ism-0263
statement: TLS traffic communicated through gateways is decrypted and inspected.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Transport Layer Security filtering

title: Control: ism-0958
statement: An organisation-approved list of domain names, or list of website categories, is implemented for all Hypertext Transfer Protocol and Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure traffic communicated through gateways.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Allowing and blocking access to domain names

title: Control: ism-1236
statement: Malicious domain names, dynamic domain names and domain names that can be registered anonymously for free are blocked by web content filters.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Allowing and blocking access to domain names

title: Control: ism-1171
statement: Attempts to access websites through their IP addresses instead of their domain names are blocked by web content filters.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Web content filters > Allowing and blocking access to domain names

title: Control: ism-0659
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content filtering checks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Performing content filtering

title: Control: ism-0651
statement: Files identified by content filtering checks as malicious, or that cannot be inspected, are blocked.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Performing content filtering

title: Control: ism-0652
statement: Files identified by content filtering checks as suspicious are quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Performing content filtering

title: Control: ism-1524
statement: Content filters used by CDSs undergo rigorous security testing to ensure they perform as expected and cannot be bypassed.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Performing content filtering

title: Control: ism-1293
statement: Encrypted files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are decrypted in order to undergo content filtering checks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Encrypted files

title: Control: ism-1289
statement: Archive files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are unpacked in order to undergo content filtering checks.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Archive files

title: Control: ism-1290
statement: Archive files are unpacked in a controlled manner to ensure content filter performance or availability is not adversely affected.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Archive files

title: Control: ism-1288
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo antivirus scanning using multiple different scanning engines.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Antivirus scanning

title: Control: ism-1389
statement: Executable files imported via gateways or CDSs are automatically executed in a sandbox to detect any suspicious behaviour.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Automated dynamic analysis

title: Control: ism-0649
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are filtered for allowed file types.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Allowing specific content types

title: Control: ism-1284
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content validation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Content validation

title: Control: ism-1965
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content checking.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Content checking

title: Control: ism-1286
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content conversion.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Content conversion

title: Control: ism-1287
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content sanitisation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Content sanitisation

title: Control: ism-0677
statement: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs that have a digital signature or cryptographic checksum are validated.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Content filtering > Validating file integrity

title: Control: ism-0591
statement: Evaluated peripheral switches are used when sharing peripherals between systems.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Peripheral switches > Using peripheral switches

title: Control: ism-1457
statement: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, or between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems belonging to different security domains, preferably complete a high assurance evaluation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Peripheral switches > Using peripheral switches

title: Control: ism-1480
statement: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems and any non-SECRET or TOP SECRET systems complete a high assurance evaluation.
group_path: Guidelines for gateways > Peripheral switches > Using peripheral switches

title: Control: ism-0663
statement: Data transfer processes, and supporting data transfer procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Data transfer processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-1535
statement: Processes, and supporting procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained to prevent AUSTEO, AGAO and REL data in textual and non-textual formats from being exported to unsuitable foreign systems.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Data transfer processes and procedures

title: Control: ism-0661
statement: Users transferring data to and from systems are held accountable for data transfers they perform.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > User responsibilities

title: Control: ism-0657
statement: When manually importing data to systems, the data is scanned for malicious and active content.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Manual import of data

title: Control: ism-1778
statement: When manually importing data to systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Manual import of data

title: Control: ism-0664
statement: Data exported from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is reviewed and authorised by a trustworthy source beforehand.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Authorising export of data

title: Control: ism-0675
statement: Data authorised for export from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is digitally signed by a trustworthy source.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Authorising export of data

title: Control: ism-0665
statement: Trustworthy sources for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are limited to people and services that have been verified and authorised as such by the chief information security officer.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Authorising export of data

title: Control: ism-1187
statement: When manually exporting data from systems, the data is checked for unsuitable protective markings.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Manual export of data

title: Control: ism-0669
statement: When manually exporting data from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, digital signatures are validated and keyword checks are performed within all textual data.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Manual export of data

title: Control: ism-1779
statement: When manually exporting data from systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Manual export of data

title: Control: ism-1586
statement: Data transfer logs are used to record all data imports and exports from systems.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Monitoring data import and export

title: Control: ism-1294
statement: Data transfer logs for systems are partially verified at least monthly.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Monitoring data import and export

title: Control: ism-0660
statement: Data transfer logs for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are fully verified at least monthly.
group_path: Guidelines for data transfers > Data transfers > Monitoring data import and export
